sample_headlines: H H Sheikha Moza Honours Over 1,100 Graduates in Doha at QF Convocation 2026
Qatar Tourism and the Qatar Foundation for Social Work sign a memorandum of understanding to promote inclusive tourism and launch the 'Autism-Friendly Spaces' initiative
Qatar Stresses Commitment to Nuclear-Weapon-Free Middle East at UN
Strait of Hormuz must remain open under all circumstances: Al Ansari
The Police Academy Launches 7th edition “Tomorrow’s Police” Summer program for Kids
Qatar’s Meteorology Department warns of strong winds, rough seas on Saturday
Qatar condemns drone attack on Kuwaiti border posts launched from Iraq
Qatar Airways set to restart flights to Dubai, Sharjah, and Damascus
Qatar steps up mosquito control with drones and fish
HH The Amir, Syrian President review regional and international developments
QCAA announces gradual resumption of foreign airlines’ services to Qatar
H.H the Amir and Pakistani PM hold talks in Doha on regional tensions and cooperation
Qatar’s food reserves in excellent condition, strategic stocks remain untouched
Qatar first 4K Business Channel "QBC" launches May 5
Qatar Airways allows free date changes for disrupted flights until October 31
Qatar denies paying to stop attacks, says civilian and infrastructure sites targeted
Ministry of Municipality launches eco-friendly fleet of vehicles
Snoonu Launches “Snoonu Cloud”; A New Platform to Power Digital Ecosystems from Qatar to the World
Qatar sends over 100 tonnes of aid to Lebanon on H.H. the Amir’s directives
Indian Minister of Petroleum and Natural Gas visits Qatar
sample_leads: Chairperson of Qatar Foundation, H H Sheikha Moza bint Nasser attended Qatar Foundation’s Convocation 2026 ceremony at Qatar National Convention Centre, where more than 1,100 graduates from its eight higher education institutions were honoured.

As part of its ongoing efforts to promote inclusive and sustainable tourism, Qatar Tourism and Qatar Foundation for Social Work have announced the signing of a strategic memorandum of understanding (MoU). The collaboration aims to support the integration of all segments of society within the tourism sector, and to create a more accommodating and comfortable environment for individuals on the autism spectrum and their families.

Qatar has renewed its commitment to advancing efforts toward a Middle East free of nuclear weapons and other weapons of mass destruction, emphasising that the complete elimination of nuclear arms remains the only guarantee against their use or threat thereof.

Qatar has reiterated firm opposition to any closure of the Strait of Hormuz, warning of severe global repercussions, while intensifying diplomatic efforts to de-escalate tensions between the United States and Iran.

The Police Academy, represented by the Police College, held a press conference today, Wednesday, May 6, 2026, to announce the opening of registration for the seventh edition of the “Tomorrow’s Police” Summer 2026 program via the Metrash app.
Qatar’s Department of Meteorology has warned of strong winds affecting both inshore and offshore areas, with rough seas expected offshore.
Qatar has condemned in the strongest terms the targeting of two locations of Kuwait’s northern land border by drones launched from Iraq.

Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of flight operations between Doha and key destinations across the Middle East, including the reinstatement of daily services to the United Arab Emirates and Syria.
Qatar is stepping up efforts to control mosquito populations through a mix of advanced technology and eco-friendly solutions, according to the Ministry of Municipality.
HH The Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani met with Syrian President Ahmed Al Sharaa at the Amiri Diwan on Wednesday.
sample_quotes: “I hope you can be agents of peace in whatever world you inherit, and may your education empower you to respond with grace and courage. The road ahead is uncertain, but then, the best roads always are,”

“Snoonu Cloud is a natural evolution of everything we’ve been building over the past years. We started by solving everyday challenges, and today we are enabling entire ecosystems to be built from the ground up. This is about taking a model created in Qatar and scaling it globally, proving that innovation built from within can compete and lead on the world stage.”

“Almeera has always been close to people’s everyday lives. Today, our focus is on strengthening that role by evolving how we serve our customers, leveraging digital tools to make their experience easier, and ensuring a consistently high standard of service.”
“At Keeta, we see our partners as more than businesses—they are part of our community,”
